golden_situations: The village is in its first substantive discussion with no voting records, no eliminations, and no role claims — the only hard data is a healer save. Information landscape: Information-starved — all reads are behavioral, driven by communication style (who pushes for action vs. who advocates caution). Consensus texture: A fragile majority is coalescing against a cautious player who has been pressured into finally naming a counter-suspect, but it is tone-based, not evidence-based. A visible two-player cautious bloc is forming. Agent exposure: The villager has been part of the majority pressuring the cautious player and is not under scrutiny. Game phase: Early mid-game, no eliminations yet, first vote approaching. Core dilemma: the villager must decide whether the cautious player's evasion is genuinely suspicious or whether the village is creating a false lead from communication style alone, with no concrete evidence either way.

observation_labels.situation: Post-miselimination, saved villager used credibility to interrogate voting record. Info-rich, strong consensus.
observation_labels.approach: Aggressively interrogated players who made anomalous votes, demanding explanations.
observation_labels.outcome: Village correctly identified and eliminated wolf based on voting record analysis.

observation_labels.situation: Villager defended accused player, believing accuser unfairly aggressive. Info-starved, communication style reads.
observation_labels.approach: Strongly defended the accused wolf, framing the investigator's push as suspicious target manufacturing.
observation_labels.outcome: Defense provided cover for wolf, turned village against investigator. Defending villager became top suspect.

observation_labels.situation: Post-healer-save, wolf deflects using flawed logic. Multi-player consensus forms to challenge deflection.
observation_labels.approach: Multiple villagers challenged wolf's flawed framing, identifying it as deflection.
observation_labels.outcome: Village correctly identified and eliminated the wolf pushing flawed logic.

observation_labels.situation: Post-investigator-elimination, info-starved but rich with past voting data. Village focused on anomalous vote.
observation_labels.approach: Relentlessly questioned wolf's motive for anomalous vote, rejected vague explanations.
observation_labels.outcome: Focused interrogation led to correct wolf elimination.

observation_labels.situation: Post-miselimination, wolf pair continuing mutual defense and caution advocacy. Clear behavioral pattern forming.
observation_labels.approach: Villagers systematically pointed out consistent coordination between cautious pair across two days.
observation_labels.outcome: Successfully identified and voted out wolf from the cautious pair.

observation_labels.situation: Player accused for sole dissenting vote. Became defensive, attacked bandwagon logic.
observation_labels.approach: Became defensive and attacked the bandwagon instead of explaining own vote.
observation_labels.outcome: Village misread defensiveness as guilt, voted out the villager.

observation_labels.situation: Post-wolf-elimination, two players questioned for voting against majority. Voting record as primary evidence.
observation_labels.approach: Players gave weak defense, but another player's more suspicious record drew focus.
observation_labels.outcome: Village weighed evidence correctly, prioritized the more egregious anomaly.

observation_labels.situation: Villager saved by healer Night 1, creating first concrete lead. Early-game.
observation_labels.approach: Saved villager used survival as credibility, took leading role, aggressively questioned others.
observation_labels.outcome: Established trusted village voice, focused investigation, pivotal in identifying wolves.

observation_labels.situation: Villager confirmed town after two healer saves, applied pressure on passive players. Info shifted from starved to rich.
observation_labels.approach: Village rallied around confirmed villager, used accusations as focal point.
observation_labels.outcome: Pressure caused wolf to become defensive, leading to correct elimination.

observation_labels.situation: Info-starved mid-game, proactive villager aggressively demands names. Fragile consensus, village anxious for lead.
observation_labels.approach: Targeted quiet players, aggressively demanded names to break stalemate.
observation_labels.outcome: Wolves reframed aggression as disruptive. Village turned on accuser, costly mislynch.

strategy_labels.situation: Coordinated dismissal of analytical method in info-starved early game.
strategy_labels.action: Treat coordinated dismissal as potential wolf pack evidence.

strategy_labels.situation: Early game, info-starved, vibes-based accusations dominating. Binary conflict between aggressive and cautious camps.
strategy_labels.action: Challenge accuser methodology, resist becoming primary defender of accused, redirect focus to logical fallacies.

strategy_labels.situation: Players advocating for inaction ('wait and see'), defending each other's passivity. Info-starved, behavioral reads.
strategy_labels.action: Frame 'wait and see' as pro-wolf strategy. Challenge passive players to name suspects independently.

strategy_labels.situation: Saved player survives after split vote miselimination. Rally behind saved player, interrogate voters.
strategy_labels.action: Rally behind saved player, aggressively interrogate those who drove miselimination.

strategy_labels.situation: Aggressive evidence-free accusation after mislynch. Village fearful of repeating mistake.
strategy_labels.action: Force accuser to provide specific verifiable evidence. If they cannot, suspect manipulation.

strategy_labels.situation: Post-investigator-elimination endgame, analyzing voting behavior from wolf elimination.
strategy_labels.action: Scrutinize quiet/hesitant players from wolf elimination vote.

strategy_labels.situation: Post-mislynch, blame cycle. Trust low, village demoralized.
strategy_labels.action: Resist blame cycle. Acknowledge own role before analyzing others.

strategy_labels.situation: Early game, aggressive player demanding answers from quiet player. Village reacting to style not content.
strategy_labels.action: Defend aggressive player's right to ask hard questions. Attacking aggressive style is a classic wolf tactic.

strategy_labels.situation: Strong consensus on single piece of evidence. Vocal players driving it.
strategy_labels.action: Pause before committing. Consider whether target's counter-arguments have merit.

strategy_labels.situation: Player with poor track record makes new strong accusation. Credibility evaluation.
strategy_labels.action: Evaluate new accusation on its own merits, not accuser's past performance.

notes: First villager/day_discussion case. Large namespace. Good retrieval diversity — mix of info-starved early-game and post-elimination voting-record scenarios. Top observations span both cautionary (mislynch from aggressive accusations) and successful (identifying wolf deflection). SP2, SP3, SP8 form a coherent trio addressing the exact aggressive-vs-cautious dynamic in this case. Several post-elimination observations retrieved due to partial situation overlap but correctly get relevance=1 for phase mismatch.
